prompt:
Implement `serialize(root: TreeNode | null): string` and `deserialize(data: string): TreeNode | null` for a binary tree. Use this node definition:
```ts
class TreeNode { val: number; left: TreeNode | null; right: TreeNode | null; constructor(val: number) { this.val = val; this.left = null; this.right = null; } }
```
The round-trip `deserialize(serialize(tree))` must reproduce the original tree structure.
canonicalSolution: class TreeNode { val: number; left: TreeNode | null; right: TreeNode | null; constructor(val: number) { this.val = val; this.left = null; this.right = null; } } export function serialize(root: TreeNode | null): string { const parts: string[] = []; function dfs(node: TreeNode | null) { if (!node) { parts.push('N'); return; } parts.push(String(node.val)); dfs(node.left); dfs(node.right); } dfs(root); return parts.join(','); } export function deserialize(data: string): TreeNode | null { const vals = data.split(','); let i = 0; function dfs(): TreeNode | null { const v = vals[i++]; if (v === 'N' || v === undefined) return null; const node = new TreeNode(Number(v)); node.left = dfs(); node.right = dfs(); return node; } return dfs(); }
test: function serRound(root: TreeNode | null): string { return serialize(deserialize(serialize(root))); } const t1 = new TreeNode(1); t1.left = new TreeNode(2); t1.right = new TreeNode(3); t1.right!.left = new TreeNode(4); t1.right!.right = new TreeNode(5); assert(serRound(t1)===serialize(t1)); assert(serialize(null)==='N'); assert(deserialize('N')===null); const single = new TreeNode(42); assert(serialize(deserialize(serialize(single)))==='42,N,N');
